shared_properties: Platform footprint stays 48x16px to match the traversal dimensions in the GDD.
Understructure inherits the dungeon stone palette used across walls and floors.
Top edge remains flat and readable so the player landing plane is obvious.
differences: Sprite sheet is 96x16px with two animation frames that gently shift moss tufts and support straps.
Moss coverage biases toward the top surface to connect it visually with the medium-friction floor family.
Underside brackets add a stronger silhouette than the static floor strips so the platform reads as mobile.

context.usage: Horizontal traversal platform that carries the player while descent continues.

shape.silhouette: Short rectangular platform with moss along the top lip and strap detail below.
shape.collision_read: Top landing plane stays flat across the full 48px frame width.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 96x16px with two 48x16px frames.
Landing edge remains flat in both frames so the movement read never changes the collision top.
The loop reads as a subtle environmental motion at 4 FPS rather than a noisy gameplay distraction.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle on a platform entity with a separate movement component.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and cache the atlas handle in the platform prefab registry.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Keep platform travel and reversal logic outside the art component so route tuning stays code-driven.
